pi_items.source: USLCI database documentation (NREL); task description explicitly states 'find an electricity-related process'. USLCI contains electricity processes with names like 'Electricity, at Grid, US' as documented on OEDI and LCA Commons.

openLCA product system naming conventions; when creating a product system from a process named 'Electricity, at Grid', the resulting product system name inherits the process name. Task description states: create product system 'from an electricity-related process'. OpenLCA product systems typically preserve the reference process name.

USLCI electricity process structure (NREL documentation); task requirement explicitly states product system should 'show connected processes in its model graph' (plural). USLCI electricity processes (e.g., 'Electricity, at grid') include upstream fuel generation (coal, natural gas, nuclear, renewables) and transmission infrastructure as linked processes. When auto-linking is enabled in openLCA, these upstream processes are automatically connected, resulting in multiple linked processes.

privileged_info_summary: This task involves the U.S. Life Cycle Inventory (USLCI) Database, a real public database of 600+ life cycle inventory processes maintained by NREL and the Federal LCA Commons. The USLCI database is based on actual US government data sources (EIA, EPA, NERC, DOE, Statistics Canada) and represents real-world inventory data, not synthetic data. The task requires creating a product system from an electricity process (e.g., 'Electricity, at grid') containing real USLCI data. The electricity process includes generation and transmission data representing the 2008 US electricity grid fuel mix (coal, natural gas, nuclear, biomass, renewables). When this process is used as a reference process with auto-linking enabled, the resulting product system will have at least 2+ linked upstream processes representing different fuel sources and transmission infrastructure. The product system's name would inherit from the electricity process name and therefore contain 'electricity'.
